@@ -0,0 +1,147 @@
+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-96
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_22-03-53+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs — added 4 tests + 3 sample-asset helpers + `SwitchableBacking` test double inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-96_TASK_2026-05-22_21-55-47+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- No non-test edits. The single touched file is
+  `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs`, and every new line is
+  inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block.
+
+### Tests
+
+Inside `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs` `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`:
+
+- Added `use std::cell::Cell;` / `use std::rc::Rc;` and
+  `use crate::animation::AnimationSampler;` imports (the new switchable
+  backing needs shared interior-mutable state across the test and the
+  boxed backing; the sample animation needs to construct a sampler with
+  a non-empty `Translation` channel).
+- Added `sample_material()` helper — `MaterialAsset::default()` with
+  `name: "negative-test-mat"` so the canonical-bytes path takes a
+  non-empty `name` prefix.
+- Added `sample_animation()` helper — `AnimationClip` with one
+  `BoneChannel::Translation` sampler, two keyframes, target node 0,
+  linear interpolation.
+- Added `sample_skeleton()` helper — `Skeleton` with name
+  `"negative-test-skel"`, three joints, no inverse-bind matrices.
+- `try_insert_material_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache`: calls
+  `try_insert_material(sample_material())` against `FailingBacking`,
+  asserts the error matches `GltfError::Cache(_)`, asserts
+  `adapter.materials.len() == 0`, and asserts
+  `adapter.get_material(&expected).is_none()` for the asset's expected
+  `content_hash()` handle.
+- `try_insert_animation_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache`: same
+  shape for `try_insert_animation` and the typed `animations` mirror.
+- `try_insert_skeleton_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache`: same
+  shape for `try_insert_skeleton` and the typed `skeletons` mirror.
+- `SwitchableBacking` test struct + `ByteCache` impl: wraps an
+  `InMemoryCache`, delegates `get` / `evict_lru` / `total_size` / `len`
+  unconditionally, and fails `put` with `CacheError::Io("synthetic:
+  switchable put failed".into())` while the shared `Rc<Cell<bool>>`
+  is `true`; otherwise forwards `put` to the inner cache. Helper is
+  scoped inside the test module, no new dev-dep.
+- `try_insert_recovers_when_backing_toggled_from_fail_to_succeed`:
+  constructs a single `AssetStoreCache::new(SwitchableBacking::new(...))`
+  with the flag initially `true`, calls `try_insert_mesh(mesh.clone())`,
+  asserts the error matches `GltfError::Cache(_)`, asserts
+  `adapter.meshes.len() == 0` and `adapter.get_mesh(&expected).is_none()`,
+  then flips the same shared flag with `flag.set(false)`, calls
+  `try_insert_mesh(mesh.clone())` again on the SAME adapter, asserts the
+  returned `MeshHandle` equals `mesh.content_hash()` exactly, asserts
+  `adapter.meshes.len() == 1`, and asserts the typed mirror now returns
+  `Some(&MeshAsset)` for the recovered handle. No adapter rebuild
+  between failure and success — only the shared flag is toggled.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None. No docs / schemas / scripts / lints / CI / gitignore touched.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+`crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs`:
+- Test module imports gained `std::cell::Cell`, `std::rc::Rc`, and
+  `crate::animation::AnimationSampler`.
+- Three new `sample_*()` helpers next to the existing `sample_mesh()`.
+- Three new `try_insert_<family>_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache`
+  tests appended after the existing `cache_error_bad_asset_id_*` test.
+- `SwitchableBacking` struct + `ByteCache` impl + the
+  `try_insert_recovers_when_backing_toggled_from_fail_to_succeed`
+  test appended at the end of the test module.
+- All edits are strictly inside `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }`. No
+  production type, public API, dependency, `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`,
+  or other-file change.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+Toolchain on this host lives at `A:\RustCache\cargo\bin` (matches the
+`.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` PATH-augment block at lines 41–56). Both
+gates were run with that PATH and `CARGO_HOME=A:\RustCache\cargo`:
+
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-io-gltf` → exit 0
+- `cargo test -p rge-io-gltf --lib --no-fail-fast asset_store_cache`
+  → exit 0. Filter ran 11 tests / 42 filtered-out; all 11 passed,
+  0 failed, 0 ignored. The 11 include all four new tests:
+  - `try_insert_material_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache` ... ok
+  - `try_insert_animation_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache` ... ok
+  - `try_insert_skeleton_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache` ... ok
+  - `try_insert_recovers_when_backing_toggled_from_fail_to_succeed` ... ok
+  …plus the seven pre-existing `asset_store_cache::tests::*` tests,
+  all still ok.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. Only
+`crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs` (test module only) and this
+execution-report packet were touched.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- The recovery test asserts both `meshes.len() == 1` and
+  `get_mesh(&h).is_some()` after the successful retry; the task lists
+  the typed-mirror check as a single MUST so either alone would
+  satisfy it. Kept both for redundancy — let me know if you'd prefer
+  the test trimmed to the single len-or-getter assertion to match the
+  pattern of the negative tests.
+- I chose `try_insert_mesh` as the family for the recovery test (the
+  task allows any `try_insert_*`). Mesh keeps the recovery test
+  symmetric with the existing
+  `try_insert_mesh_surfaces_backing_error_as_gltf_cache` test that
+  already exists in the file.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs`
+  (uncommitted; the test-module edit is the only diff).
+- Untracked items:
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-96_TASK_2026-05-22_21-55-47+0300.md`
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-96_TASK_2026-05-22_21-55-47+0300.meta.json`
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-96_EXEC_2026-05-22_22-03-53+0300.md` (this report)
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-96`
+- Last commit: `db8ed81` chore(dispatch): tighten task #6 recovery-test
+  MUST to name switchable backing + content_hash assertion
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-96
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,98 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-96
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-22_21-55-47+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Add focused test-only coverage for `AssetStoreCache::try_insert_*` backing-write failures in `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs`. The uncovered material, animation, and skeleton fallible insert paths must prove that backing `put` errors surface as `GltfError::Cache` and leave the typed mirror unchanged. Add one recovery test that uses the same switchable backing across a failing call and a later successful call, proving the failure is transactional and the recovered handle is exactly the asset's `content_hash()` handle.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs`, only inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block.
+- The executor's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-96_EXEC_*.md` execution report packet.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- Any non-test code in `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs`.
+- Any file outside `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs`, except the executor's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-96_EXEC_*.md` report packet.
+- `Cargo.toml`.
+- `Cargo.lock`.
+- Any source file in another crate.
+- Any documentation, schema, script, lint, CI, or gitignore file.
+- Any existing handoff packet other than this dispatch's required execution report.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-96_EXEC_*.md`, created with `new-handoff.ps1` for the execution report.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- No new Rust modules, integration tests, fixtures, snapshots, documentation, scripts, dependencies, or lockfile artifacts.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Add a `try_insert_material` negative test in the existing `asset_store_cache.rs` test module.
+- Add a `try_insert_animation` negative test in the existing `asset_store_cache.rs` test module.
+- Add a `try_insert_skeleton` negative test in the existing `asset_store_cache.rs` test module.
+- Each new negative test must use a backing that fails `put`, assert the result is `GltfError::Cache`, and assert the corresponding typed mirror was not updated after the failure.
+- Add one switchable-backing recovery test in the same test module. It must use one `AssetStoreCache` instance with the same backing toggled from failing to succeeding across `try_insert_*` calls, then assert the successful returned handle equals `asset.content_hash()`.
+- Add any small test-only sample asset helper functions or test backing structs needed for those tests, scoped inside the existing test module.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- All new code is inside `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs` under the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`.
+- There is a distinct negative test for each currently uncovered family: material, animation, and skeleton.
+- The material failure test asserts `adapter.materials.len() == 0` and/or `adapter.get_material(&asset.content_hash()).is_none()` after the failed `try_insert_material` call.
+- The animation failure test asserts `adapter.animations.len() == 0` and/or `adapter.get_animation(&clip.content_hash()).is_none()` after the failed `try_insert_animation` call.
+- The skeleton failure test asserts `adapter.skeletons.len() == 0` and/or `adapter.get_skeleton(&skel.content_hash()).is_none()` after the failed `try_insert_skeleton` call.
+- The recovery test does not rebuild the adapter between failure and success. It toggles one backing instance, such as with `Cell<bool>` or `AtomicBool`, from fail to succeed.
+- The recovery test first observes a failed `try_insert_*` result and verifies the typed mirror remains empty or missing for the expected handle.
+- The recovery test then toggles the same backing to success, calls the same fallible insert family again, and asserts the returned handle is exactly equal to the asset's `content_hash()` handle.
+- The recovery test verifies the typed mirror contains the asset after the successful retry.
+- No production behavior, public API, dependency, or Cargo metadata changes are made.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This is a test-only dispatch.
+- Do not change `AssetStoreCache`, canonical byte helpers, handle types, asset structs, or the `Cache` trait implementation.
+- Do not add new dev-dependencies; use only standard library types and dependencies already available to `rge-io-gltf`.
+- Do not move these tests to integration tests or another module.
+- Do not broaden coverage beyond the issue goal except for minimal helper code needed by the requested tests.
+- Do not rewrite unrelated existing tests. Existing tests may be left as-is unless a tiny in-module helper reuse edit is needed.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --check -p rge-io-gltf` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-io-gltf --lib --no-fail-fast asset_store_cache` -> expected exit 0, including the new material, animation, skeleton, and recovery tests.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- The requested tests cannot be added entirely inside the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block in `crates/io-gltf/src/asset_store_cache.rs`.
+- Making the tests pass appears to require modifying production code or public API behavior.
+- Making the tests pass appears to require editing `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, or adding a dependency.
+- The switchable backing recovery test cannot be implemented without rebuilding the adapter between the failing call and the successful retry.
+- Any verification failure cannot be resolved within the allowed test-only edit scope.
+- The executor discovers pre-existing unrelated failures that block verification; report them in the execution packet rather than widening scope.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+Existing context: `asset_store_cache.rs` already has `FailingBacking` plus mesh and image failure tests. Reusing `FailingBacking` for the three new negative tests is acceptable. The new recovery test likely needs a separate switchable backing that delegates successful operations to `InMemoryCache` and fails `put` only while a shared flag is set. Keep that helper private to the test module.
+
+Suggested sample assets can be minimal: `MaterialAsset::default()` with a distinguishing name, an `AnimationClip` with either no samplers or one simple sampler, and a `Skeleton` with a small joint list. The key assertion is not sample richness; it is transactional mirror behavior and exact handle equality with `content_hash()`.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-96
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+NEXT_ROLE: EXECUTOR_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---
